page: Microsoft Azure B16pls_v2 is a burstable virtual machine belonging to the Bpsv2 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an ARM64 architecture powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor. This processor operates at 3.0 GHz and provides 16 physical cores and 16 vCPUs, with a single thread per core configuration. The server is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

Performance benchmarks place this server in the average tier for most multi-core and memory operations. The stress-ng multi-core CPU benchmark scores 28890.73, while the single-core test scores 1822.69. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 1126.95 GB/sec, dropping to 133.81 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Cryptographic and single-threaded decompression tasks show weaker performance, with the Passmark CPU encryption test scoring 2592.74 and bzip3 single-threaded decompression reaching 8.7 GB/sec. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performs at an average level of 73.54 GB/sec.

Given its burstable CPU allocation and ARM architecture, the B16pls_v2 offers a cost-effective profile for workloads with variable CPU utilization. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it suitable for scale-out applications that do not require continuous maximum CPU performance. Appropriate workloads include static web serving, where it achieves a throughput of 18.67 GB/sec, database operations, and software compilation tasks. It is less suited for intensive cryptographic applications or large-scale LLM inference due to its weaker single-core and specialized instruction performance.
description: Microsoft Azure B16pls_v2 is a burstable virtual machine from the Bpsv2 family, powered by an ARM64 Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz. It features 16 physical cores, 16 vCPUs, and 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a 2.0 GB per core memory allocation. The server does not include local storage or GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmark results indicate average multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 28890.73 and a static web serving throughput of 18.67 GB/sec, though single-core cryptographic and decompression tasks perform weakly. This burstable resource profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads with fluctuating demands, making it well-suited for web serving, software compilation, and lightweight database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B16pls_v2 is a burstable ARM64 virtual machine featuring 16 Ampere Neoverse-N1 vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM, optimized for cost-effective web serving and variable workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure B16pls_v2 is a burstable ARM64 virtual machine with 16 vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM, designed for variable workloads.
tagline: A burstable ARM64 virtual machine offering sixteen cores and balanced multi-threaded performance for variable scale-out workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 16-core Ampere Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor running at 3.0 GHz
Equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing 2.0 GB of memory per core
Burstable CPU allocation designed for cost-effective handling of variable workloads
Supports Premium Storage with no local storage or integrated GPUs
Suited for static web serving, software compilation, and database operations
